summary: Short, lighthearted Cavallo blog post offering humorous spring riding tips for horse owners heading into riding season.
Barefoot hoofcare practitioner Marianne Allen recounts trimming Mouche, a foundered dressage horse with rotated coffin bones, and pairing care with Cavallo hoof boots.
Recap of America's Favourite Trail Horse event in Blanco, Texas, where Cavallo served as major co-sponsor celebrating trail-horse soundness.
Rider Donna Jensen's testimonial on transitioning her reining quarter horse Tippy to barefoot after meeting Cavallo's Carole at a trail-horse event.
Excerpt from Yvonne Welz of The Horse's Hoof exploring what truly defines a barefoot horse and barefoot owner within the barefoot movement.
Spring horse-care tips from vet Dr. Hermen Geertsema covering spring grass and laminitis risk, grazing muzzles, and safely legging horses back into shape.
First progress report on transitioning quarter horse Tippy to barefoot, documenting trimmer Luke Tanner's work and Tippy's early adjustment.
Guest blogger Dutch Henry introduces his monthly Cavallo column sharing inspirational stories of horses and the people they help.
Guest post by Joe Camp (author of Benji and The Soul of a Horse) reflecting on letting horses graze naturally on spring and Bermuda grasses.
Part two of Yvonne Welz's Barefoot Paradigm, laying out the four-step philosophy of identifying and removing causes of equine hoof and health problems.
Recap of Cavallo's visit to the Western States Horse Expo in Sacramento, highlighting events, distributors, and rescue partners.
Brief congratulatory message from Cavallo to graduates, with a special note to Eali and a Ralph Waldo Emerson quote.
